window_summary: This window tracks the recent aristocratos/btop release stream from v1.1.0 through v1.4.7, covering OS support additions, GPU monitoring, new themes, and numerous bug fixes.

chunks.title: btop++ (v1.1.0) OSX Support
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: aristocratos/btop
Release: btop++ (v1.1.0) OSX Support
Tag: v1.1.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2021-11-14T00:23:19Z; created_at: 2021-11-14T00:23:12Z
Release notes:
## Changelog v1.1.0
* Added: Support for OSX, by @joske and @aristocratos
**For additional binaries see the [Continuous Builds](https://github.com/aristocratos/btop/actions).**
**Linux binaries for each architecture are statically linked with musl and works on kernel 2.6.39 and newer.**
**Notice! Use x86_64 for 64-bit x86 systems, i486 and i686 are 32-bit!**
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the aristocratos/btop release v1.1.0, the project adds support for OSX, contributed by @joske and @aristocratos.

chunks.title: btop++ (v1.1.1) OSX build fixes
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: aristocratos/btop
Release: btop++ (v1.1.1) OSX build fixes
Tag: v1.1.1; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2021-11-16T07:41:22Z; created_at: 2021-11-16T07:41:16Z
Release notes:
## Changelog v1.1.1
* Added: Partial static build (libgcc, libstdc++) for macos
* Changed: Continuous build macos switched to OSX 11.6 (Big Sur) and partial static build
* Changed: Release binaries for macos switched to OSX 12 (Monterey) and partial static build
**For additional binaries see the [Continuous Builds](https://github.com/aristocratos/btop/actions).**
**Linux binaries for each architecture are statically linked with musl and works on kernel 2.6.39 and newer.**
**Macos binaries are statically linked with libgcc and libstdc++ but only guaranteed to work on the OSX version mentioned in the name.**
**Notice! Use x86_64 for 64-bit x86 systems, i486 and i686 are 32-bit!**
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the aristocratos/btop release v1.1.1, the project adds partial static build (libgcc, libstdc++) for macOS, switches continuous build macOS to OSX 11.6 (Big Sur) and partial static build, and switches release binaries for macOS to OSX 12 (Monterey) and partial static build.

chunks.title: btop++ (v1.1.2) Bug fixes
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: aristocratos/btop
Release: btop++ (v1.1.2) Bug fixes
Tag: v1.1.2; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2021-11-27T17:52:22Z; created_at: 2021-11-27T17:52:10Z
Release notes:
## Changelog v1.1.2
* Fixed: SISEGV on macos Mojave, by @mgradowski
* Fixed: Small optimizations and fixes to Mem::collect() and Input::get()
* Fixed: Wrong unit for net_upload and net_download in config menu
* Fixed: UTF-8 detection on macos
* Fixed: coretemp iteration due to missing tempX_input, by @KFilipek
* Fixed: coretemp ordering
**For additional binaries see the [Continuous Builds](https://github.com/aristocratos/btop/actions).**
**Linux binaries for each architecture are statically linked with musl and works on kernel 2.6.39 and newer.**
**Macos binaries are statically linked with libgcc and libstdc++ but only guaranteed to work on the OSX version mentioned in the name.**
**Notice! Use x86_64 for 64-bit x86 systems, i486 and i686 are 32-bit!**
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the aristocratos/btop release v1.1.2, the project fixes a SIGSEGV on macOS Mojave, small optimizations and fixes to Mem::collect() and Input::get(), wrong unit for net_upload and net_download in config menu, UTF-8 detection on macOS, coretemp iteration due to missing tempX_input, and coretemp ordering.

chunks.title: btop++ (v1.1.3) New themes + bugfixes
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: aristocratos/btop
Release: btop++ (v1.1.3) New themes + bugfixes
Tag: v1.1.3; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2021-12-11T17:22:16Z; created_at: 2021-12-11T17:22:08Z
Release notes:
## Changelog v1.1.3
* Added: New theme ayu, by @AlphaNecron
* Added: New theme gruvbox_dark_v2, by @pietryszak
* Fixed: Macos cpu coretemp for Intel, by @joske
* Added: New theme OneDark, by @vtmx
* Fixed: Fixed network graph scale int rollover
* Fixed: Suspected possibility of very rare stall in Input::clear()
**For additional binaries see the [Continuous Builds](https://github.com/aristocratos/btop/actions).**
**Linux binaries for each architecture are statically linked with musl and works on kernel 2.6.39 and newer.**
**Macos binaries are statically linked with libgcc and libstdc++ but only guaranteed to work on the OSX version mentioned in the name.**
**Notice! Use x86_64 for 64-bit x86 systems, i486 and i686 are 32-bit!**
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the aristocratos/btop release v1.1.3, the project adds new themes ayu, gruvbox_dark_v2, and OneDark, fixes macOS CPU coretemp for Intel, fixes network graph scale int rollover, and fixes a suspected possibility of very rare stall in Input::clear().

## Changelog v1.2.0
* Added: Support for FreeBSD, by @joske and @aristocratos
* Fixed (again): Account for system rolling over net speeds in Net::collect()
* Added: Theme gruvbox_material_dark, by @marcoradocchia
* Added: Option for base 10 bytes/bits

**Notice! Use x86_64 for 64-bit x86 systems, i486 and i686 are 32-bit!**
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the aristocratos/btop release v1.2.0, the project adds support for FreeBSD, fixes (again) accounting for system rolling over net speeds in Net::collect(), adds theme gruvbox_material_dark, and adds option for base 10 bytes/bits.

chunks.title: btop++ (v1.2.1) Bug fixes
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: aristocratos/btop
Release: btop++ (v1.2.1) Bug fixes
Tag: v1.2.1; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2022-02-11T19:02:27Z; created_at: 2022-02-11T19:02:21Z
Release notes:
## Changelog v1.2.1
* Added: Arrow only after use of "f" when filtering processes, by @NavigationHazard
* Fixed: Fx::uncolor not removing all escapes
* Fixed: Text alignment for popup boxes
* Fixed: Terminal resize warning getting stuck
* Removed: Unnecessary counter for atomic_lock
* Added: Percentage progress to Makefile
* Fixed: Alignment of columns in proc box when wide UTF8 characters are used
* Fixed: Battery meter draw fix
**For additional binaries see the [Continuous Builds](https://github.com/aristocratos/btop/actions).**
**Linux binaries for each architecture are statically linked with musl and works on kernel 2.6.39 and newer.**
**RISCV64 Linux binaries currently unavailable while we investigate compilation issues with musl, manual compilation with dynamic linking still works.**
**Macos binaries are statically linked with libgcc and libstdc++ but only guaranteed to work on the OSX version mentioned in the name.**
**Notice! Use x86_64 for 64-bit x86 systems, i486 and i686 are 32-bit!**
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the aristocratos/btop release v1.2.1, the project adds arrow only after use of "f" when filtering processes, fixes Fx::uncolor not removing all escapes, fixes text alignment for popup boxes, fixes terminal resize warning getting stuck, removes unnecessary counter for atomic_lock, adds percentage progress to Makefile, fixes alignment of columns in proc box when wide UTF8 characters are used, and fixes battery meter draw.

**Notice! Use x86_64 for 64-bit x86 systems, i486 and i686 are 32-bit!**

chunks.title: btop++ (v1.2.7) Bug fixes & new features

Release: btop++ (v1.2.7) Bug fixes & new features
Tag: v1.2.7; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2022-05-28T19:08:36Z; created_at: 2022-05-28T19:08:30Z
Release notes:
## Changelog v1.2.7
* Fixed: Disk IO stats for individual partitions instead of whole disk (Linux)
* Added: Case insensitive process filtering, by @abrasumente233
* Added: Include ZFS ARC in cached/available memory on Linux, by @mattico
* Added: Desktop entry and icons, by @yonatan8070
* Fixed: Net sync scale bug
* Added: tokyo-night & tokyo-storm themes, by @Schievel1
**For additional binaries see the [Continuous Builds](https://github.com/aristocratos/btop/actions).**
**Linux binaries for each architecture are statically linked with musl and works on kernel 2.6.39 and newer.**
**Macos binaries are statically linked with libgcc and libstdc++ but only guaranteed to work on the OSX version mentioned in the name.**
**Notice! Use x86_64 for 64-bit x86 systems, i486 and i686 are 32-bit!**
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the aristocratos/btop release v1.2.7, the project fixes disk IO stats for individual partitions instead of whole disk (Linux), adds case insensitive process filtering, adds inclusion of ZFS ARC in cached/available memory on Linux, adds desktop entry and icons, fixes net sync scale bug, and adds tokyo-night & tokyo-storm themes.

chunks.title: btop++ (v1.2.9) Bug fixes + new features and themes
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: aristocratos/btop
Release: btop++ (v1.2.9) Bug fixes + new features and themes
Tag: v1.2.9; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2022-08-28T11:47:45Z; created_at: 2022-08-28T11:47:37Z
Release notes:
## Changelog v1.2.9
* Fixed: Memory values not clearing properly when not in graph mode in mem box
* Changed: kyli0x theme color update, by @kyli0x
* Added: Elementarish theme, by @dennismayr
* Added: key "?" to see help, by @mohi001
* Added: solarized_light theme, by @Fingerzam
* Changed: Made ZFS stats collection compatible with zfs_pools_only option, by @simplepad
* Changed: Rewrite of process sorting and tree generation including fixes for tree sorting and mouse support
* Added: Option to hide the small cpu graphs for processes
* Changed: Small graphs now show colors for each character
* Fixed: Getting selfpath on macos (fix for finding theme folder)
**For additional binaries see the [Continuous Builds](https://github.com/aristocratos/btop/actions).**
**Linux binaries for each architecture are statically linked with musl and works on kernel 2.6.39 and newer.**
**Macos binaries are statically linked with libgcc and libstdc++ but only guaranteed to work on the OSX version mentioned in the name.**
**Notice! Use x86_64 for 64-bit x86 systems, i486 and i686 are 32-bit!**
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the aristocratos/btop release v1.2.9, the project fixes memory values not clearing properly when not in graph mode in mem box, changes kyli0x theme color update, adds Elementarish theme, adds key "?" to see help, adds solarized_light theme, changes to make ZFS stats collection compatible with zfs_pools_only option, rewrites process sorting and tree generation including fixes for tree sorting and mouse support, adds option to hide the small CPU graphs for processes, changes small graphs to show colors for each character, and fixes getting selfpath on macOS (fix for finding theme folder).

chunks.title: btop++ (v1.2.10) Bug fixes, cleanup + a new theme
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: aristocratos/btop
Release: btop++ (v1.2.10) Bug fixes, cleanup + a new theme
Tag: v1.2.10; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2022-10-06T20:16:46Z; created_at: 2022-10-06T20:16:34Z
Release notes:
## Changelog v1.2.10
* Fixed: Process tree filtering not case insensitive
* Added: Paper theme, by @s6muel
* Fixed: Extra checks to avoid crash on trying to replace empty strings in tree mode
* Fixed: Crashing when cores are offline
* Fixed: Cpu::collect() core count counter...
* Changed: Using sysconf(_SC_NPROCESSORS_CONF) for number of cores instead of sysconf(_SC_NPROCESSORS_ONLN)
* Maintenance: Code cleanup, by @stefanos82
**For additional binaries see the [Continuous Builds](https://github.com/aristocratos/btop/actions).**
**Linux binaries for each architecture are statically linked with musl and works on kernel 2.6.39 and newer.**
**Macos binaries are statically linked with libgcc and libstdc++ but only guaranteed to work on the OSX version mentioned in the name.**
**Notice! Use x86_64 for 64-bit x86 systems, i486 and i686 are 32-bit!**
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the aristocratos/btop release v1.2.10, the project fixes process tree filtering not case insensitive, adds Paper theme, fixes extra checks to avoid crash on trying to replace empty strings in tree mode, fixes crashing when cores are offline, fixes Cpu::collect() core count counter, changes to using sysconf(_SC_NPROCESSORS_CONF) for number of cores instead of sysconf(_SC_NPROCESSORS_ONLN), and performs code cleanup.

chunks.title: btop++ (v1.2.13) Bug fixes
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: aristocratos/btop
Release: btop++ (v1.2.13) Bug fixes
Tag: v1.2.13; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2022-11-06T21:36:41Z; created_at: 2022-11-06T21:36:34Z
Release notes:
## Changelog v1.2.13
* Makefile: VERBOSE=true flag for Makefile to display all compiler commands and fixed so already set CXXFLAGS and LDFLAGS are displayed.
* Makefile: Added autodetection for gcc12 to make compiling on macos Ventura easier.
* Changed: Reverted back to sysconf(_SC_NPROCESSORS_ONLN) for Cpu core count ant let the new dynamic update fix if cores are turned on later
* Fixed: Ignore disks that fails in statvfs64() to avoid slowdowns and possible crashes.
* Fixed: Moved up get_cpuHz() in the execution order to get better cpu clock reading.
* Added: proc tree view: if there's more than 40 width left, try to print full cmd, by @Superty
* Fixed: Show the first IP of the interface in NET box instead of the last, by @correabuscar
* Changed: Replace getnameinfo with inet_ntop [on Linux], by @correabuscar
* Fixed: Not picking up last username from /etc/passwd
* Fixed: Process nice value underflowing, issue #461
* Changed: Replace getnameinfo with inet_ntop [on FreeBSD], by @correabuscar
* Changed: Replace getnameinfo with inet_ntop [on macos], by @correabuscar
Edit:
* Changed: Release packages folder structure, all files now contained in `btop` folder, for reason see #242
**For additional binaries see the [Continuous Builds](https://github.com/aristocratos/btop/actions).**
**Linux binaries for each architecture are statically linked with musl and works on kernel 2.6.39 and newer.**
**Macos binaries are statically linked with libgcc and libstdc++ but only guaranteed to work on the OSX version mentioned in the name.**
**Notice! Use x86_64 for 64-bit x86 systems, i486 and i686 are 32-bit!**
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the aristocratos/btop release v1.2.13, the project adds VERBOSE=true flag for Makefile to display all compiler commands and fixes display of already set CXXFLAGS and LDFLAGS, adds autodetection for gcc12 to make compiling on macOS Ventura easier, reverts back to sysconf(_SC_NPROCESSORS_ONLN) for CPU core count and lets the new dynamic update fix if cores are turned on later, fixes ignoring disks that fail in statvfs64() to avoid slowdowns and possible crashes, moves up get_cpuHz() in the execution order to get better CPU clock reading, adds proc tree view: if there's more than 40 width left, try to print full cmd, fixes showing the first IP of the interface in NET box instead of the last, replaces getnameinfo with inet_ntop on Linux, fixes not picking up last username from /etc/passwd, fixes process nice value underflowing, replaces getnameinfo with inet_ntop on FreeBSD, replaces getnameinfo with inet_ntop on macOS, and changes release packages folder structure so all files are now contained in btop folder.

chunks.title: btop++ (v1.3.0) GPU Support Linux and platform support OpenBSD
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: aristocratos/btop
Release: btop++ (v1.3.0) GPU Support Linux and platform support OpenBSD
Tag: v1.3.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2024-01-07T14:23:07Z; created_at: 2024-01-07T14:23:01Z
Release notes:
## Changelog v1.3.0
* Added Gpu Support Linux | @romner-set | PR #529
* Added platform support for OpenBSD | @joske | PR #607
* Enable macos clang | @muneebmahmed | PR #666
* Fix Apple Silicon CPUs misprinted | @masiboss | PR #679
* Cmake support for MacOS | @imwints | PR #675
* Elementarish theme: color update according to Elementary palette | @stradicat | PR #660
* Add alternative key codes for Delete, Insert, Home, End | @ivanp7 | PR #659
* Fix scrollbar not clearing sometimes. | @DecklynKern | PR #643
* Add keybind for toggling memory display mode in PROC box | @rahulaggarwal965 | PR #623
* Minor string initialization improvement | @imwints | PR #636
* Made disks statvfs logic asynchronous. | @crestfallnatwork | PR #633
* Fix signal list on non-linux/weird linux platforms | @lvxnull | PR #630
* Add option to accumulate a child's resources in parent in tree-view | @imwints | PR #618
* Add CMake support for Linux | @imwints | PR #589
* Horizon theme | @SidVeld | PR #610
* Fix short conversion of 1000-1023 *iB | @scorpion-26 | #609
* Fix integer overflows in btop_collect.cpp | @dorrellmw | #546
* Support compiling with LLVM | @imwints | #510
* Fix getting zfs pool name with '.' char in freebsd | @jfouquart | #602
* [macos/freebsd] support gcc13 | @joske | #600
* FreeBSD swap info | @rrveex | #560
* Create adwaita.theme | @flipflop133 | #485
* Try get terminal size of "/dev/tty" if stdout fails | @imwints | PR #627
* Refresh rate program argument | @imwints | PR #640
* Improved error handling when determining the config directory | @imwints | #652
* Use native POSIX polling syscalls to read input | @lvxnull | #624
* Conditional compile on Big Sur and up | @joske | PR #690
* + Various fixes by @imwints, @simplepad, @joske, @gwena, @cpalv, @iambeingtracked, @mattico, @NexAdn
**For additional binaries see the [Continuous Builds](https://github.com/aristocratos/btop/actions).**
**Linux binaries for each architecture are statically linked with musl and works on kernel 2.6.39 and newer.**
**No Macos and FreeBSD binaries provided for the moment.**
**Notice! None of the binaries have GPU support, compile yourself or wait for distribution packages for GPU monitoring support!**
**Notice! Use x86_64 for 64-bit x86 systems, i486 and i686 are 32-bit!**
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the aristocratos/btop release v1.3.0, the project adds GPU support for Linux, adds platform support for OpenBSD, enables macOS clang, fixes Apple Silicon CPUs misprinted, adds CMake support for macOS, updates Elementarish theme color according to Elementary palette, adds alternative key codes for Delete, Insert, Home, End, fixes scrollbar not clearing sometimes, adds keybind for toggling memory display mode in PROC box, makes minor string initialization improvement, makes disks statvfs logic asynchronous, fixes signal list on non-linux/weird linux platforms, adds option to accumulate a child's resources in parent in tree-view, adds CMake support for Linux, adds Horizon theme, fixes short conversion of 1000-1023 *iB, fixes integer overflows in btop_collect.cpp, supports compiling with LLVM, fixes getting zfs pool name with '.' char in freebsd, supports gcc13 on macOS/FreeBSD, adds FreeBSD swap info, creates adwaita.theme, tries to get terminal size of "/dev/tty" if stdout fails, adds refresh rate program argument, improves error handling when determining the config directory, uses native POSIX polling syscalls to read input, and conditionally compiles on Big Sur and up.

chunks.memory_unit_text: In the aristocratos/btop release v1.4.1, the project includes various code fixes, fixes a typo, moves the config parser into its own module, adds a menu option to show bitrates in base 10 separate from the setting to show bytes/bits in base 10, allows MidnightBSD to build btop using the existing FreeBSD support, uses XDG_STATE_HOME to save logs, bumps CMake version to 3.25 required for LINUX variable, replaces brackets with arrows in net and proc box, bumps bundled fmt to 11.1.4, links to CMAKE_DL_LIBS in cmake, fixes phoenix-night.theme marked as executable, adds Kanagawa-lotus and Kanagawa-wave themes, bumps NetBSD version to 10.1 and FreeBSD version to 14.2, adds dark version of adwaita theme: adwaita-dark, resets last selection on page navigation in optionsMenu to avoid unordered_map error, shares the CPU name trimming code between platforms, updates Ryzen name trimming, drops macOS 12 build and adds v14 and v15, fixes cmake-macos workflow, bumps version of deprecated upload-artifact step, updates obsolete egrep call, fixes menu crash when GPU_SUPPORT=false, adds 'Everforest Light Medium' theme, supports Intel GPUs before Gen-6 (patch from upstream), fixes SEGFAULT in intel_name_lookup_shim.c, fixes rsmi_measure_pcie_speeds not saving, and shows GPU Watt fractions when below 100W.

#1401 | Feat: add mouse dragging of scrollbar | @yinghao-w
#1399 | feat: add cli option to dump default config | @deckstose
e969f43 | Added "save_config_on_exit" option to configuration | @aristocratos
Big thanks to @deckstose for helping out with project maintenance, PR reviews and merging!
**For additional binaries see the [Continuous Builds](https://github.com/aristocratos/btop/actions).**
**Linux binaries for each architecture are statically linked with musl and works on kernel 2.6.39 and newer.**
**No MacOs or BSD binaries provided for the moment.**
**Notice! None of the binaries have GPU support, compile yourself or wait for distribution packages for GPU monitoring support!**
**Notice! Use x86_64 for 64-bit x86 systems, i586 and i686 are 32-bit!**
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the aristocratos/btop release v1.4.6, the project skips 'H' and 'HX' suffix in AMD CPU name trimming, adds process renice functionality, reverts a disk I/O change, introduces CPU frequency display modes, fixes CPU frequency detection for LoongArch CPUs, fixes arrow keys that send 'O' instead of '[', adds OpenBSD battery support for watthour and watts sensors, adds Gotham theme, fixes macOS deadlocks and race conditions in collector threads, fixes btop crash on ARM CPU, adds ability to set which GPU vendors to enable, adds tree toggle of expand/collapse for children of process, uses right key codes to match F1 and F2 keys, handles missing SMC gracefully on macOS, fixes redraw in proc list when changing sort order, fixes load average and GPU watts leaking past box border, respects effective CPUs from cgroup, displays container engine, raises C++ standard to C++23, adds configuration toggle for terminal synchronization, implements process list pausing, flips name/command/user sorting, fixes tty theme for pause banner, adds test harness with ctest, fixes skipping first argument of argv, adds --themes-dir flag, fixes over-/underflow when parsing --update argument, fixes segfault on musl systems, passes only static duration strings to Config::set in Config::apply_preset, fixes CPU per-thread totals not reaching 100%, aligns boxes when CPU bottom and GPU on, includes minor fixes and improvements for NetBSD, fixes GPU rendering, always collects GPU data when cpu_graph_lower = "Auto", fixes humanized units shortening, adds mouse dragging of scrollbar, adds CLI option to dump default config, and adds "save_config_on_exit" option to configuration.

chunks.title: btop++ (v1.4.7) New features & Bug fixes
chunks.raw_text:

Release: btop++ (v1.4.7) New features & Bug fixes

## Changelog v1.4.7
References | Description | Author(s)
--- | --- | ---
#1326 | Feat: Process following and following mode improvements | @vandabbin

#1583 | fix: correctly map amd zen cpu core temps | @sola-contrib
#1628 | fix: restore Intel Mac CPU temperature fallback | @seanniu93
#1560 | fix: Use SVG features compatible with Qt SVG Tiny 1.1 | @ratijas
ea977a0 | Update widechar detection and fix replace_ascii_control() removing wide characters | @aristocratos
7657384 | Fix cpu watt overflow in cpu box + division by zero fix for cpu.temp_max | @aristocratos
b36357b | Fixed missing CPU package temperature color (numeric value) | @aristocratos
Big thanks to @deckstose for helping out with project maintenance, PR reviews and merging!

**No MacOs or BSD binaries provided for the moment.**
**Notice! None of the binaries have GPU support, compile yourself or wait for distribution packages for GPU monitoring support!**
**Notice! Use x86_64 for 64-bit x86 systems, i586 and i686 are 32-bit!**
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the aristocratos/btop release v1.4.7, the project adds process following and following mode improvements, adds Apple Silicon GPU support via IOReport API, adds Kanagawa Dragon theme, adds twilight theme, adds swap upload/download speed graph option and fixes network graphs overlap, adds Flexoki dark and light themes, adds menu option to disable presets, creates orange.theme, adds E hotkey to collapse/expand all processes in tree mode, fixes restoring Apple Silicon temp reporting on M2 Pro, fixes regression from a switch to kIOMainPortDefault, fixes hide and info buttons, fixes mouse mapping for process kill buttons with vim keys enabled, fixes kill buttons, fixes following mode, fixes and improves pause and follow modes, removes unused asprintf implementation function, removes busy waiting when checking runner thread state, updates clock with preset change or show/hide box, tweaks RX/TX collection and drawing and fixes -Wparentheses warning, fixes mem bottom border when net is not shown, fixes force_tty menu option sets TTY mode on start, fixes accumulating pwr_usage into pwr_total instead of mem_total in Gpu::collect(), fixes reading GPU/VRAM clocks when ROCm SMI reports version 1.x, fixes rendering non-ascii chars in proc list, fixes CPU mib queries for OpenBSD and only displays online CPUs in output, fixes correctly mapping AMD Zen CPU core temps, fixes restoring Intel Mac CPU temperature fallback, fixes using SVG features compatible with Qt SVG Tiny 1.1, updates widechar detection and fixes replace_ascii_control() removing wide characters, fixes CPU watt overflow in CPU box and division by zero fix for cpu.temp_max, and fixes missing CPU package temperature color (numeric value).

questions.query_text: In the aristocratos/btop releases, which release adds support for OSX?
Across the aristocratos/btop releases, how does the GPU-related change in v1.3.0 differ from the GPU-related change in v1.4.0?

options.text: v1.3.0 adds GPU support for Linux, while v1.4.0 adds Intel GPU support.
v1.3.0 adds Intel GPU support, while v1.4.0 adds GPU support for Linux.
Both releases add the same GPU support for AMD GPUs.
v1.3.0 removes GPU support, while v1.4.0 restores it.

questions.expected_answer: v1.3.0 adds GPU support for Linux, while v1.4.0 adds Intel GPU support.

questions.query_text: In the aristocratos/btop release window, order the following release-content states from earliest to latest: (1) a release that adds support for FreeBSD, (2) a release that adds GPU support for Linux, (3) a release that adds Intel GPU support, (4) a release that adds Apple Silicon GPU support via IOReport API.

answer_support.answer_text: adds Apple Silicon GPU support via IOReport API
